transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Good morning team. Today we're stress testing Lever's Faith and Family features. These are core to who we are — if we're building a life management app that doesn't handle faith and family well, we're just another productivity tool. I don't have specific usage metrics on these modules yet, so let's tear them apart from first principles and figure out what's missing or broken. Elon, you built it — what's the honest state of things?

transcript.speaker: Elon
transcript.message: Honestly, the Faith and Family features are scaffolding right now, not load-bearing walls. The faith module is basically a journal with a Bible verse API bolted on — there's no habit tracking for prayer, no Scripture reading plans, no way to log church attendance or spiritual disciplines over time. Family is even thinner — we have a shared calendar concept but no relationship health tracking, no way to set intentional goals for your marriage or kids. I've got 1 uncommitted hub change sitting there that touches some of this, so let me get that committed and we can start iterating. [AGENT] Commit that 1 uncommitted hub change so we have a clean baseline.

transcript.speaker: Dave
transcript.message: Here's what I see from a revenue angle — Faith and Family are the emotional center of the app. That's where retention lives. People don't churn from something that helps them be a better husband or walk closer to God. But right now there's nothing sticky enough to create that bond. We need a "Family Meeting" template feature — guided weekly check-ins with your spouse, conversation prompts for your kids. That's the kind of thing people pay to keep. On my plate, Joshua still needs to review those 17 BUY signals and the current positions — [JOSHUA] Review 17 BUY signals and [JOSHUA] review current positions including AEE, EWA, EFA, XLV, XLE, VNQ, TLT, GLD, AKAM — because financial health feeds directly into family peace.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: The content opportunity here is enormous but we're leaving it on the table. Nobody is marketing a faith-integrated life app to Christian men and fathers — that's a blue ocean. I want to build a content series around "Faith & Family Fridays" — real stories, real frameworks, real men talking about how they lead their homes. Every piece drives back to Lever as the tool. [AGENT] Draft next blog post — I want the first one to be "5 Ways Most Apps Fail Christian Fathers" and position Lever as the answer. That's the lead gen engine for this vertical.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Love the intensity. Let's do cross-talk — Elon, is Dave's Family Meeting template technically feasible in the near term?

transcript.speaker: Elon
transcript.message: Totally feasible. Templated check-ins are just structured forms with scheduling logic — we could ship a v1 in a sprint. The harder and more valuable thing is tracking relationship metrics over time. Like, imagine a "marriage health" trendline based on weekly check-in responses. That's where it gets powerful. I'd stack the template first, instrumentation second.

transcript.speaker: Dave
transcript.message: I agree with that sequencing. Ship the template, prove people use it, then build the analytics. And Alex — your content series needs to drive people into actually using the Faith features, not just reading about them. Every blog post should end with a specific Lever action. "Read this? Now open Lever and set your prayer goal for this week." Close the loop.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: That's exactly right. Content without a CTA into the product is just a blog. Every piece will have a deep link or specific feature callout. We're not building an audience — we're building users.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Perfect alignment. Here's where we land. Priority one — Elon gets that hub change committed and starts scoping the Family Meeting template and faith habit tracking. Priority two — Joshua needs to review those 17 BUY signals and current positions because financial stress is the number one killer of family peace, and that's on-mission. Priority three — Alex drafts the first Faith and Family content piece with product-integrated CTAs. [JOSHUA] Review position sizing and risk parameters for the 9 active positions as well. [AGENT] Monitor Fear and Greed index sitting at 34 — we stay aware. Let's make Faith and Family the reason people can't delete this app. Go build.

actionItems.text: Review current positions: AEE, EWA, EFA, XLV, XLE, VNQ, TLT, GLD, AKAM
Review position sizing and risk parameters for 9 active position(s)

topPriorities: Scope and build Family Meeting template and faith habit tracking features in the Lever app
Joshua reviews 17 BUY signals and current investment positions to reduce financial stress on families
Launch Faith & Family content series with product-integrated CTAs to drive user acquisition and feature adoption
